questions.body: What is the function of a gene in the context of heredity?

questions.body: How does antiretroviral therapy (ART) help manage HIV?

snippets.text: antiretroviral therapy (ART) helps manage HIV by reducing the viral load in the body, improving immune function, and preventing the progression to AIDS.

questions.body: What are the early symptoms of HIV infection?

snippets.text: early symptoms of HIV infection can include fever, fatigue, swollen lymph nodes, and rash, which are often mistaken for other viral infections.

questions.body: What kind of virus targets the immune system?

snippets.text: HIV is a virus that specifically targets and weakens the immune system, making it harder for the body to fight infections.

questions.body: How does HIV impact the immune system?

snippets.text: HIV attacks and destroys CD4 cells, weakening the immune system and making the body more susceptible to infections and certain cancers.

questions.body: What is the role of post-exposure prophylaxis (PEP) in HIV prevention?
